task: Implement the following plan: # Plan: Simplify Assets Table — Remove 4 Columns ## Context The assets table at `/assets` has too many columns, making it visually cluttered. The user wants to remove **Description**, **Criticality**, **Type**, and **Created** columns from the table view. ## File to Modify - `src/frontend/src/components/AssetManagement.tsx` ## Changes ### 1. Remove column headers (lines 826–827, 831, 833) Remove these `` elements from the ``: - `Type` (line 826) - `Criticality` (line 827) - `Description` (line 831) - `Created` (line 833) ### 2. Remove column data cells in `` Remove corresponding `` elements for each row: - **Type** cell (lines 873–875): `...` - **Criticality** cell (line 876): `{getCriticalityBadge(...)}` - **Description** cell (line 888): `{asset.description || '-'}` - **Created** cell (lines 900–902): `{asset.createdAt ? ...}` ### 3. Remove dead code The `getCriticalityBadge` helper function (lines 839–868) becomes unused after removing the Criticality column — remove it entirely. ## Remaining Columns After Change Name | IP Address | AD Domain | Owner | Workgroups | Actions ## Verification - Run `npm run build` in `src/frontend/` to verify no build errors - Visually confirm the table only shows: Name, IP Address, AD Domain, Owner, Workgroups, Actions If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/flake/.claude/projects/-Users-flake-sources-misc-secman/e03bd841-8fc8-4d2b-b734-b9cd98aff506.jsonl
